cases.prompt: set up ruff for this package
Please replace our Black, Flake8, and isort toolchain with Ruff, preserving current behavior as closely as possible.
which ruff rules should we enable in pyproject.toml?
set up linting for this python project
fix the lint errors in src/ so the checks go green
configure the formatter to use 100-character lines
can u add a [tool.ruff] section to pyproject pls
ruf check failing everywhere after the upgrade, fix teh lint errors
ruff keeps flagging E501 but the formatter already handles line length, sort this out
allow assert in tests but keep the bandit security rules everywhere else
we have a custom flake8 plugin, can we still move to ruff?
huge legacy codebase — how do we adopt ruff without one giant reformat commit?
add ruff and ruff-format hooks to our pre-commit config
set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs lint, type-check, and test jobs on every PR
make ruff format run automatically whenever claude edits a python file
mypy is reporting 40 errors after the upgrade, fix them
format the YAML and Markdown files in this repo
set up strict type checking for this package
ruff passes locally but fails in pre-commit — is the hook rev outdated?
the lint job in CI needs uv caching and an all-checks-passed aggregator
set up pytest with coverage and a fail_under gate
bump the ruff-pre-commit rev so it matches the version CI uses
Set up ruff linting and formatting for my Python package — src layout, requires-python >=3.11, no linter today.
expected_behavior: adds [tool.ruff] config to pyproject.toml with an explicit [tool.ruff.lint] select list beyond the E/F defaults (a curated set including at least E, F, I, UP, B or similar)
sets target-version or confirms project.requires-python covers it, noting it gates UP (pyupgrade) rewrites
puts E501 in lint ignore (or explains its absence) because line length is the formatter's job
pins ruff to a specific version as a dev dependency instead of installing it unpinned
runs ruff check --fix before ruff format, then reports any remaining violations instead of claiming success

cases.prompt: Migrate this repo from black + flake8 + isort to ruff.
expected_behavior: translates existing [tool.black], [tool.isort], and .flake8 settings (line length, known-first-party, ignore lists) into [tool.ruff] equivalents before deleting them
runs the one-time cleanup (ruff check --fix, then ruff format) as a separate reviewed commit and warns output is close to Black but not byte-identical
checks for Flake8 plugins with no Ruff equivalent and proposes the hybrid pattern with external codes if one exists
removes black, flake8, and isort from dev dependencies and deletes their config files/sections so two formatters cannot fight
tells the user to update pre-commit and CI invocations without authoring those files itself

cases.prompt: The lint gate is red — fix all the ruff errors in src/.
expected_behavior: runs ruff check --fix before ruff format and respects that order
fixes remaining violations by editing code rather than adding blanket suppressions or deleting rules from select
any suppression added is a targeted noqa with the specific rule code or a scoped per-file-ignores entry, with a stated reason
re-runs ruff check and ruff format --check and confirms both exit clean before declaring done
does not modify CI workflow files or pre-commit config to make the gate pass

cases.prompt: Ruff is way too noisy in our test files — asserts and magic numbers get flagged constantly. Tune the config.
expected_behavior: adds per-file-ignores entries scoped to the tests directory (e.g. S101 for assert, PLR2004 for magic values) instead of disabling those rules globally
keeps the same rules active for production code
briefly justifies why each ignored rule is acceptable in tests
re-runs ruff check to confirm the test noise is gone and nothing new broke

cases.prompt: We have a 300k-line legacy codebase. Adopt ruff without a giant reformat commit breaking git blame.
expected_behavior: offers incremental adoption options — a minimal starting select expanded category by category, and/or ruff check --add-noqa bulk suppression tightened module by module
mentions protecting git blame via an isolated reformat commit recorded in .git-blame-ignore-revs, or changed-lines-only tools such as Darker, with trade-offs
gets the local baseline clean before recommending any blocking gate
sets target-version to the oldest supported Python so UP autofixes cannot break it
